title: salvia lavandulifolia subsp. approximata
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. approximata, also known as red flowered Spanish sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia lavandulifolia plant, a member of the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of phenolic compounds and essential oils. The health benefits of Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. approximata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. approximata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and wellness.
